ACT _ONE FADE IN: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - KITCHEN Scene 2,

MARGE is in the kitchen making dinner. We see her shaking a wide variety of spices onto some breaded pork chops. HOMER is looking over her shoulder, admiring her handiwork. We can hear Bart and Lisa LAUGHING 0.S. MARGE (LOW TO HERSELF) A dash of rosemary, a smidgen of thyme, a pinch of marjoram... HOMER You know, Marge, you make the best pork chops in the whole world. MARGE (GENUINE MODESTY) Oh now, Homer, they're nothing special. The extra ingredient is care. Marge continues spicing the pork chops. MARGE (CONT'D) A sprinkle of chervil, half a teaspoon of turmeric...

Marge takes a garlic clove, puts it in her mouth and chews. She puffs on the pork chops.

REVISED TABLE DRAFT 6/21/90 PAGE 2. Scene *\ MARGE (CONT'D) CeonT'D) «ss and a whisper of garlic. HOMER Marge, I'm gonna build you a spice rack! MARGE You don't have to go to all that trouble. HOMER It's no trouble. I've got a whole garageful of tools I never use. Homer exits. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM Bart, Lisa and Maggie are watching TV. ON TV KRUSTY is looking through a spyglass. KRUSTY Hey, kids! I spy Itchy and Scratchy off the port bow! (KRUSTY LAUGH) We see the opening of the Itchy & Scratchy Show. Itchy and Seene® Scratchy are running towards the camera POUNDING each other é over the head with mallets as the THEME SONG plays. ITCHY AND SCRATCHY CHORUS "They fight, they bite / they fight and bite and fight / fight, fight, fight / bite, bite, bite / The Itchy and Scratchy show."

On the couch we see Maggie watching with wide-eyed fascination.

KIDS' Pov - TV seene*z We see Homer, from chest to knees, walk through the f.g. BART (0.S.) Down in front! HOMER Shut up, boy. SUPER: "HOLD THAT FELINE" Scratchy is waiting for the kick-off on a football field. Itchy lights a fuse in a teed-up football and kicks off. Scratchy fields the ball and there's a huge EXPLOSION, leaving a crater in the field. A scorched, blackened Scratchy falls into the hole and is immediately jumped on by an entire football team coming from all directions. INT. GARAGE - CONTINUOUS Homer crosses the obviously unused garage that's packed with obviously unused tools, many with the original plastic still covering them. He walks to a shelf that has every handyman encyclopedia ever made. HOMER (TO HIMSELF) I knew all this stuff would come in handy some day. Let's see now... ah, here it is. The Complete Handyman's Bookshelf. Volume One. Spice Racks. Homer takes the volume off the shelf and puts it on his unused workbench. He crosses to a small pile of unused wood, selects one that still has the small red flag on it, lays it on the workbench and opens the spice rack book to page one. HOMER (CONT'D) (READING) "Pick - up - a - ham - mer..."

He picks up a hammer. It has a price tag dangling from it.

SAME SCENE - HALF HOUR LATER Homer has nailed a number of small pieces of wood together in a form that could pass for a spice rack. He frequently consults the book for reference. There is a pile of discarded wood on the floor with nails sticking out of each piece at crazy angles. Homer is sweating and MUTTERING.

DISSOLVE TO: CLOSE-UP - SPICE RACK IN BOOK

We PULL OUT to Homer holding up his finished spice rack. It looks terrible.

HOMER (CONT'D)

(AWED BY HIS ACHIEVEMENT) Wowww.... Suddenly we HEAR PSYCHO-TYPE MUSIC stings. NEW ANGLE Maggie is on the stairway above Homer's head. She is holding a large mallet. As the PSYCHO MUSIC continues, she bashes Homer over the head with all her might.

DISSOLVE TO:

EXTREME CLOSEUP - HOMER'S EYE

In a move exactly like the end of the shower scene in "Psycho,"

CAMERA MOVES OUT to Homer's lifeless body which is sprawled on the garage floor. We hear a deep dull TRAGIC STING and the scene slowly fades. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM = LATER THAT EVENING Homer is lying on the couch, his head bandaged. Large quantities of blood have dried on the bandages. Marge is fussing with his pillow. Bart and Lisa are sitting on the couch too, trying to see around Homer's swollen head so they can watch TV.

BART

Hey, Dad. Can you move your head?

Scratchy is pinned to a butcher block with knives through his clothing. Itchy is trying to stab him, but Scratchy's chest keeps moving sideways to avoid being hit. We start to hear the PSYCHO STINGS again, but they stop when Marge picks Maggie up and takes away the pencil. MARGE So, that's where she gets it! No, Maggie! Bad baby! Marge turns off the TV. BART/LISA We were watching that! MARGE Well, you won't be watching these cartoons anymore. Ever. LISA But, Mom! If you take our cartoons away, we'll grow up without a sense of humor and be robots. BART (TO LISA. INTERESTED) Really? What kind of robots? EXT. SCHOOLGROUNDS - NEXT MORNING Scenets Bart is walking up to the monkeybars, where MILHOUSE is hanging upside down eating part of his lunch. crosses to Bart and puts a sympathetic hand on his shoulder.

Homer is laying down on the couch surrounded by empty plates, beer cans, and snack product bags. He's talking on the phone.

Because I said so. HOMER Because you said so, why? MARGE (FRUSTRATED) Homer! I'm trying to work! HOMER Oh. (BEAT) What are you doing? MARGE I'm cataloguing the violence in these cartoons. I don't think adults have ever actually sat down and watched them before. ON TV Itchy hits Scratchy in the back of the head with a sledgehammer. Scratchy's eyes fly out. Scratchy staggers around looking for them. Itchy gives Scratchy two HISSING bombs. Scratchy forces the bombs into his eyesockets, then goes over to a mirror and begins combing his hair. He sees his reflection in the mirror, notices the bombs and runs around SCREAMING until he EXPLODES. BACK TO HOMER AND MARGE MARGE What kind of warped human being would find that funny? HOMER

Heh, heh, heh.

Other boxes that have already been checked include "Mice Launched," "Dogs Tricked," "Gophers Buried Alive," "Eyes Knocked Out," "“Disembowelings" and "Brains Slammed In Car Door." MARGE (SHAKING HER HEAD) This is the kind of entertainment they think is suitable for younger and more impressionable viewers? HOMER (CHUCKLES AT SOMETHING ON TV) Yeah, but what are ya gonna do? MARGE (SUDDEN RESOLUTION) I'll tell you what I'm going to do. I'm going to put a stop to it. HOMER You are? When? MARGE Right now. (DRAMATICALLY) Homer, I'm going to write a letter. Marge goes over to the desk and sits down. She takes out a

sheet of writing paper that is headed "From the Mind of Marge" and begins writing.

MARGE SceNE*y (WRITING) "Dear Purveyors of Senseless Violence: JI know this may sound silly

at first..."

CeenT'd) KRUSTY is holding Marge's letter at various angles, MUMBLING, trying to figure out what the letter might mean.

INT. TV STUDIO - KRUSTY'S DRESSING ROOM

MARGE (0.S.) (CONT'D) "... but I believe that the cartoons you show to our children are influencing their behavior in a negative way..."

Frustrated, Krusty crumples up the letter and throws it away.

KRUSTY (FRUSTRATED) Eh... EXT. ITCHY & SCRATCHY INTERNATIONAL - FRONT GATE We see a mammoth factory, with tall stacks belching black smoke. The massive wrought iron gates say "Itchy & Scratchy International." The logo features a wrought iron cat chopping a mouse in half vertically with an axe. The camera MOVES IN slowly through the bars. INT. I & S INTERNATIONAL - ANIMATOR'S OFFICE An ANIMATOR, who has an office with a window, is reading Marge's letter. Crude drawings of Itchy and Scratchy destroying each other line the walls of his office. There are also several tacky looking awards. MARGE (0.S.) (CONT'D) "... please try to tone down the psychotic violence..."

He crumples up the letter, without emotion, and throws it into a basketball hoop wastebasket.

seme*7 (cont'd) ROGER MYERS, a large round bald man with a permanent frown

INT. CHAIRMAN I & S INTERNATIONAL'S OFFICE

MARGE (0.S.) (CONT'D ",.. in your otherwise fine programming. Yours truly, Marge Simpson."

He puts down the letter and thinks for a moment. His frown seems to deepen and become a little more permanent.

ROGER MYERS Scenetg Take a letter, Miss White. (DICTATING) "Dear Valued Viewer: Thank you for taking an interest in the Itchy and Scratchy Program. Enclosed is a personally autographed photo of America's favorite cat and mouse team to add to your collection." CLOSE-UP - PHOTO Itchy is driving a steamroller. Scratchy has one foot caught beneath it and reacts in terror. The photo is

signed: "Yours truly" with a paw print and "Best Wishes" with a smaller paw print.

Scones ROGER MYERS (CONT'D) Ccent'p) "In regards to your specific comments about the show, there is no real need for us to respond, because our research indicates that one person cannot make a difference, no matter how big a screwball she is. Marge angrily crumples up the letter and the photo. MARGE (CONT'D) I'll show them what one screwball can do! FADE OUT:

ACT TWO Scene *4

FADE IN: EXT. ITCHY & SCRATCHY INTERNATIONAL - FRONT GATE Marge, Homer, Maggie, Bart, and Lisa are all picketing. Marge is carrying a sign that says "I'm protesting because Itchy & Scratchy are indirectly responsible for my husband being hit on the head with a mallet." This sign is very hard to read from any distance and curious PASSERSBY are forced to stand very close to the sign and squint. Homer is carrying a sign that says "Please ban violent cartoons. Next time I might not be so lucky." Maggie is waving a little sign like a pennant. It says "Stop me before I kill my father again." Bart and Lisa are both unhappily carrying signs that say "Ban Itchy & Scratchy." Bart surreptitiously writes in the word "Don't" at the top of his sign and looks less unhappy. HOMER (LOW, TO MARGE) Everybody's looking at us, Marge. MARGE That's what we want them to do, Homer. HOMER Oh. (BEAT) How long are we going to be doing this? MARGE Well, I've never changed the world before, so I don't know how long it takes exactly. But if enough people take an interest...

MAUDE FLANDERS stops, looks at Marge, then looks at a large free-standing sign near Homer. It says "Join SNUH NOW!"

MAUDE

Hello, Marge! What is "SNUH?"

REVISED TABLE DRAFT 6/21/90 PAGE 15. Sceeve Hq MARGE GonT'd) It stands for "Springfieldians for Nonviolence, Understanding and Helping." I've started a crusade against cartoon violence. I can protect my own children, but there are many others whose minds are being warped every afternoon at four. Bart hears this and looks at his watch. BART That reminds me. I've gotta get over to Milhouse's and... uh... play sports. MARGE All right. Bart puts down his sign. Lisa puts hers down too. LISA And I'm going over to Janey's again. We're going to be... um... making the most of our childhood years. MARGE Have fun. BART/LISA We will.

They hurry off, stifling guilty GIGGLES. Maude Flanders picks up Lisa's sign and joins the demonstration.

SAME SCENE ON TV SCREEN ene Fy PULL BACK TO INT. ITCHY & SCRATCHY INTERNATIONAL - BOARDROOM The BOARD MEMBERS, Roger Myers, and Krusty are watching the tape of the show on a bank of televisions. On the many screens, Krusty is still LAUGHING nervously, sweating buckets as the audience continues to CHANT. The chairman hits a button and all the sets go blank. MYERS (GRUFFLY) That woman. That screwball, Marge Simpson. We've got to stop her.

But how?

No one says anything. All the board members look at each other.

MYERS (CONT'D) (ANGRILY) How??!!?? ANIMATOR (QUICKLY) Drop an anvil on her? 1sT BOARD MEMBER Hit her on the head with a piano? 2ND BOARD MEMBER Stuff her full of TNT, then throw a match down her throat and run? MYERS All your fancy degrees and that's the best you can do?

Silence. Several members shift in their chairs uneasily.

Itchy and Scratchy stop clouting each other and look down at the squirrel. Itchy swings his bat and knocks the squirrel's head out of the ballpark. The crowd CHEERS. CLOSE-UP - KENT BROCKMAN Scene */é BROCKMAN Hello, I'm Kent Brockman, and welcome to another edition of "Smartline". (BEAT) Are cartoons too violent for children? Most people would say "no. Of course not. What kind of stupid question is that?" But one woman says "yes." She's here with us tonight. Mrs. Marge Simpson. CLOSE-UP - MARGE SUPER: "MARGE SIMPSON. FOUNDER OF S.N.U.H." LISA (POINTING) There she is!

Mortises of the participants in the discussion come up on screen. Note: We should parallel the look of "NIGHTLINE."

INT. TV STUDIO - SET OF "SMARTLINE" CAMERA PANS — PANEL BROCKMAN Also with us for this "animated" discussion... (CHUCKLES)... are Roger Myers, Chairman of the board of Itchy & Scratchy International... Roger Myers nods to the camera. BROCKMAN (CONT'D) ++. Krusty the Klown, whose delightful program brings us Itchy & Scratchy

every afternoon at four...

Seene “7 KRUSTY Coon!) (KRUSTY LAUGH) SFX: HORN HONK BROCKMAN And, joining us live via satellite from Vienna, the home of Sigmund Freud - the world's most famous psychiatrist - to give us an insight into the human mind, Dr. Marvin Monroe. We see MARVIN MONROE on a monitor. He nods. MARVIN MONROE Hiya. BROCKMAN Now, first let's see what all the excitement is about. Here's an example of the work currently being turned out at I & S Studios. All turn to look at the monitor. Marvin Monroe also turns. ON TV CLOSE-UP of Scratchy's grave. It says "Scratchy. Rest In Peace." There are wires leading away from the grave. CAMERA PANS along the wires until it comes to Itchy, who winks at the camera and then presses the plunger on the detonator. Scratchy's grave BLOWS UP, throwing Scratchy's SCREAMING, burning skeleton comically high into the air. BACK TO PANEL

Brockman is wiping tears from his eyes.

REVISED TABLE DRAFT 6/21/90 PAGE 25.

BROCKMAN (CONT'D) Hilarious. (TURNS TO MARGE) Now, what in the world is wrong with that, Mrs. Simpson?

MARGE (UNCOMFORTABLE, EYEING CAMERA) Well... I think it's a bad influence on children.

MYERS (A LITTLE AWKWARD) I think that is baloney, Kent. And here's why. In preparing for this debate, I did a little research and I discovered a startling thing. There was violence in the past, long before cartoons were invented.

BROCKMAN (LIKE STEVE ALLEN) I see. Fascinating.

MYERS There was something called the Crusades, for instance. Tremendous violence. Many people killed.

BROCKMAN And this was before cartoons were

invented?

Scene *q MYERS Ceont’ D) That's right, Kent. (TO MARGE) So much for your viewpoint. MARGE (SNORTS) BROCKMAN Dr. Monroe in Vienna, would you care to comment? MARVIN MONROE Scenetie

‘VE FROM VIENNA DR. MONROE Well, Kent... to me, the shenanigans or... high jinks - call them what you will - of a few comic characters pale in comparison with the crippling emotional problems a psychiatrist runs into every day. I'm referring to women who love too much, fear of winning, sexaholism... stuff like that. BROCKMAN So you have no professional objection

to Itchy & Scratchy?

REVISED TABLE DRAFT 6/21/90 PAGE 27.

céne**| DR. MONROE cel Not at all. In fact, one of my guilty little pleasures is to get a big bucket of popcorn, dim the lights, turn on Itchy & Scratchy, and laugh myself silly. And what the hell is wrong with that? BACK TO PANEL BROCKMAN (CHUCKLING) Not a thing. Now, for another opinion... CAMERA PANS TO KRUSTY Scene*q

Krusty immediately starts bouncing around in his seat and mugging for the camera.

KRUSTY (KRUSTY LAUGH) Hi kids!!!

BROCKMAN (CHUCKLES) Krusty, please. We're giving you an opportunity to participate in a serious discussion.

Krusty settles down.

KRUSTY (APOLOGETICALLY) I'm sorry, Kent. It's just that when the camera gets on me... (NOTICES CAMERA)... HEY HEY!!!

Krusty starts capering around, HONKING his horn and blindsides himself with a pie.

REVISED TABLE DRAFT 6/21/90 PAGE 28. Seens#q BROCKMAN (BARKING) Krusty!

Krusty's smile fades. He sits back down and looks sullenly at the table.

BROCKMAN (CONT'D) Well, it looks like we're not going to settle this tonight. Mrs. Simpson, in the nine seconds we have left, would you like to summarize your position? CLOSE-UP = MARGE MARGE Yes. I'd like to ask all the parents in Springfield who are concerned about this to write in and let the cartoon makers know how you feel. Thank you. INT. The phone RINGS. Marge picks up the kitchen extension, props the receiver between her shoulder blade and neck and continues breading the pork chops. MARGE (INTO PHONE) Hello? INT. I & S INTERNATIONAL - ROGER MYERS' OFFICE Myers, surrounded by frustrated ANIMATORS and STORYBOARD ARTISTS, sits at a drawing board, looking at a half completed storyboard. MYERS (INTO PHONE) Okay then, you're so smart, how do we end this picture? BACK TO MARGE She stops breading the pork chops and listens, baffled. MARGE (INTO PHONE) Well... what's the

problem you're having?

Scratchy is understandably upset. And we figured that Scratchy - the protagonist - could grab Itchy and toss him in a bucket of acid. BACK TO MARGE MARGE (INTO PHONE) Oh dear! BACK TO MYERS MYERS (INTO PHONE) But then we remembered that this might be interpreted as violence which is morally wrong now, thanks to you. So what's your big

Myers hangs up the phone, picks up his pencil and begins sketching in Marge's idea.

The bell RINGS. The children look up, but then continue working. MRS. HOOVER (FIRMLY, BUT PUZZLED) Children... I'm sorry, but the bell has rung. School is over. You can learn more tomorrow. INT. ART MUSEUM - FLORENCE, ITALY SUPER: "FLORENCE, ITALY" Scene #24

WORKMEN are putting Michelangelo's David into a huge orange crate for shipping. The museum's CURATOR is speaking to a small group of REPORTERS.

Stene*z7 CURATOR Ceontto)

(ITALIAN ACCENT) This will be the art event of the century. The greatest masterpiece of the Italian Renaissance, Michelangelo's David, on a coast-to- coast tour of the United States. REPORTER Which cities will be included in your itinerary? CURATOR New York... The reporters nod and write this down. CURATOR (CONT'D) -- Springfield...

We hear an OMINOUS MUSIC STING as the reporters nod and write this down.

caption underneath the photograph says "Michelangelo's David in 1958."

work.

Marge is awakened by a NOISY crowd on her front lawn and an insistent POUNDING on the front door.

MARGE (SLEEPILY) What in the world? She puts on a robe and hurries downstairs. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - FOYER Marge opens the door. Dozens of members of her protest group are standing on the walk waving anti-nudity signs.

Many of the signs are just the old anti-violence signs with references to nudity painted in.

REVISED TABLE DRAFT 6/21/90 PAGE 41. Scene ¥ 24 HELEN LOVEJOY Ceont'd) Get dressed. You've got to lead our protest against this abomination!

Helen hands Marge a newspaper. We see it is last night's paper with the story about the statue coming to town.

MARGE (MYSTIFIED) That's Michelangelo's David. It's a masterpiece! HELEN LOVEJOY (CORRECTING HER) It's filth. It graphically portrays parts of the human body which - practical as they may be... are evil! MARGE But I like that statue. I think it's good. HELEN LOVEJOY It's not. MAUDE FLANDERS Who needs you? Come on, girls. Unhappily, Marge watches them go. EXT. SPRINGFIELD AIRPORT The crate holding David is on the tarmac just below the cargo door of a jumbo jet. Several AIRLINE OFFICIALS are looking at it doubtfully, and POLICEMEN are trying to hold back the surging CROWD of sign-carrying protesters. The Curator seems unable to make sense of what is going on. CURATOR What is all this about? What do you

people want?

The CAMERA PANS today's panel. It is comprised of Marge, the curator, the statue - out of its crate and wearing a pair of slacks - and Dr. Marvin Monroe with the ruins of the Parthenon behind him. There is a SUPER: Live via satellite from Athens, Greece. Monroe is wearing a white tank top, zinc oxide on his nose and a Greek fisherman's cap on his head. BROCKMAN Is it a masterpiece? Or just some guy with his pants down? That's our topic tonight on "Smartline". (TURNS TO MARGE) Now, Mrs. Simpson, why are you against this statue? MARGE I'm not. I think everyone in Springfield should see it. BROCKMAN Wait a minute. Aren't you Marge Simpson, the wacko? MARGE

(MURMURS) Hmmm... yes and no.
